title: Pseudo vs True Randomness & EML Complexity

key_theorem.statement: A sequence s = (s_n) is pseudo-random iff its minimal EML depth is finite. PRNGs are EML-2: their closed-form generator is x_n = f(n) for EML-2 f. True random sequences (thermal noise, quantum, Chaitin Ω) are EML-∞: no EML-finite function generates them.
key_theorem.corollary_csprng: CSPRNGs exhibit an EML depth paradox: structural depth is EML-2 (polynomial key map) but computational depth is EML-∞ (no poly-time inversion). This is the EML interpretation of one-way functions.
key_theorem.corollary_chaos: Deterministic chaotic systems transition from EML-2 (short time) to effective EML-∞ (long time) at the Lyapunov scale. This is the same EML-∞ transition as phase transitions (Session 57) and PDE blowup (Session 62).

lcg.eml_class: EML-2: LCG closed form x_n = f(n)
lcg.reason: a^n = exp(n·ln a) is EML-2; full LCG recurrence is EML-2

mersenne_twister.reason: MT state update is a linear recurrence: x_n = M^n·x_0 over GF(2). M^n symbolically = exp(n·ln M) → EML-2. Empirically passes all statistical tests, but structurally EML-2.
mersenne_twister.note: MT is not cryptographically secure: given 624 outputs, future outputs are predictable.

csprng.paradox: CSPRNG is EML-2 structurally (polynomial map of key+counter) but EML-∞ computationally (no poly-time algorithm identifies the EML-2 form). This is the EML interpretation of one-way functions and computational pseudorandomness.
csprng.eml_one_way_principle: A function f is one-way iff its EML depth (structural) is finite but its computational EML depth (to invert) is effectively EML-∞.

randomness_spectrum.theorem: EML Randomness Theorem: A sequence s = (s_n) is pseudo-random iff there exists k < ∞ such that s_n = round(f(n)) for some EML-k function f. A sequence is truly random iff no EML-finite function generates it, i.e., the minimal EML depth of any generating function is EML-∞.

spectrum.statistical_quality: passes basic tests, fails lattice test
passes all known tests, cryptographically secure
spectrum.eml_generator: ARX polynomial map = EML-2 structurally; EML-∞ computationally
No finite EML tree; left-computable only
No hidden variable EML-finite generator (Bell theorem)

periodic_r3.2.reason: Short time: recurrence x_n = f^n(x_0) is a rational function → EML-2. Long time: chaos → Lyapunov divergence exp(λt) → sensitive dependence → effective EML-∞ (no finite-depth tree predicts x_n for large n without full precision).

chaotic_r3.9.reason: Short time: recurrence x_n = f^n(x_0) is a rational function → EML-2. Long time: chaos → Lyapunov divergence exp(λt) → sensitive dependence → effective EML-∞ (no finite-depth tree predicts x_n for large n without full precision).

feigenbaum_transition.connection: Same EML-∞ transition as phase transitions (Session 57) and NS blowup (Session 62)

eml_depth_summary.EML-2: LCG, Mersenne Twister, CSPRNG (structurally), logistic map (short time)
eml_depth_summary.EML-3: π digits (conjectured normal; BBP formula is EML-3)
eml_depth_summary.EML-\u221e: True thermal noise, quantum randomness (QRNG), Chaitin Ω, logistic map (long time)

connections.to_session_57: Phase transition EML-∞ = same barrier as deterministic chaos onset
connections.to_session_70: Quantum QRNG = EML-∞ via Bell theorem = same class as thermal noise
connections.to_complexity_theory: One-way functions = EML-2 structurally, EML-∞ computationally
